file_analyses.file_purpose: Test data file containing comprehensive country and locale mapping configuration for the Instant Ink application. Maps country names to their ISO codes, supported languages, geographic regions (NA, EU, APJ), and card payment gateway assignments (PGS or 2CO). Used throughout test flows to configure region-specific test execution, determine payment gateway routing, and validate locale-specific functionality across 38 countries in North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific regions.

data_structure.description: Dictionary mapping country names to locale configuration objects

fields.description: Array of supported language codes for this country
Geographic region code - NA (North America), EU (Europe), or APJ (Asia-Pacific Japan)
Payment gateway system used for card payments - PGS (Payment Gateway Service) or 2CO (2Checkout)

usage_examples: from core.utils import load_json_file; locale_data = load_json_file('test_flows_common/locale_map.json'); us_config = locale_data['United States']
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a critical test data configuration file that centralizes all country/locale information for the Instant Ink test automation framework. It provides a single source of truth for country codes, language support, regional groupings, and payment gateway routing. The data structure is well-organized with consistent field naming across all country entries. The file supports 38 countries across three major regions (NA, EU, APJ) and two payment gateway systems (PGS and 2CO). Several countries support multiple languages (Canada with en/fr, Belgium with fr/nl, Switzerland with de/fr/it, Hong Kong with zh/en). This data is likely consumed by test configuration logic to determine which payment methods are available, which language variants to test, and how to route payment transactions based on the test country. The payment gateway split (PGS vs 2CO) appears to follow a pattern where Western European countries and North America use PGS, while Eastern European countries and Asia-Pacific use 2CO.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test data file containing Instant Ink subscription offer configurations organized by country code. Each offer includes type (free or prepaid), unique GUID identifier, offer identifier code, payment requirement flag, and pricing in cents. Used by test flows to select appropriate offers for enrollment testing based on country and offer type, supporting both free trial offers and prepaid subscription offers across multiple countries.

data_structure.description: Dictionary mapping country codes to arrays of offer objects

offers.description: Free 1-month trial offer requiring additional payment method
$5.00 prepaid offer requiring additional payment method
€5.00 prepaid offer requiring additional payment method

field_definitions.description: Offer type - free trial or prepaid subscription
Unique identifier for the offer in UUID format
Offer identifier code used for API requests
Whether additional payment method is required even for prepaid offers
Offer price in cents (for prepaid offers)
Duration in months (for free trial offers)

usage_examples: from core.utils import load_json_file; offers = load_json_file('test_flows_common/offers.json'); us_offers = offers['US']
free_offer = [o for o in offers['US'] if o['type'] == 'free'][0]
prepaid_offers = [o for o in offers['DE'] if o['type'] == 'prepaid']
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test data file provides offer configurations for enrollment testing across 14 countries. The United States has the most diverse offer set with 5 different offers including 1 free trial and 4 prepaid options at various price points ($3, $5, $30, $60). Germany also has 2 offers. Most European countries (AT, BE, ES, FI, FR, IE, IT, LU, NL, PT) share a common prepaid offer with GUID 02ba58f3-8f8a-48d5-8d99-16559ee18ed1, identifier 10232, and amount 6000 cents (€60). The additionalPaymentRequired flag indicates whether a payment method must be provided even for prepaid offers - this is true for the US free trial and some prepaid offers, likely for recurring billing after the initial period. The identifier field is used to generate offer codes via API calls (see offer_request function in test_flows_common.py). Pricing is stored in cents to avoid floating-point precision issues. The GUID serves as the unique offer identifier in the backend system while the identifier is used for code generation. This data structure supports the get_offer and get_offer_code functions in test_flows_common.py which filter offers by type and amount criteria.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test data file containing payment method credentials and configuration for automated payment testing. Includes test credit card numbers for major card networks (Mastercard, Visa, Amex, Discover), IBAN details for direct debit testing, PayPal test account credentials, and Google Pay test credentials. Each payment method includes all necessary fields for form filling and transaction simulation including card numbers, CVV codes, expiration dates, 3DS authentication results, and emulated payment outcomes.

data_structure.description: Dictionary mapping payment method identifiers to payment credential objects

fields.description: Expiration year as offset from current year (current year + 6)
Test IBAN for German bank account
Alternative PayPal test account for UK

payment_methods.description: Empty object for prepaid-only offers that don't require payment method

special_features.description: Expiration year uses '+6' notation to calculate 6 years from current year at runtime
special_features.implementation: Handled by get_payment_method_data() function in test_flows_common.py

special_features.description: Credit cards include expected 3DS authentication result for secure payment testing
Direct debit and Google Pay include emulated_result field for transaction simulation
Multiple test accounts provided for direct debit and PayPal to support parallel testing

usage_examples: # Dynamic year calculation handled automatically: card_data = get_payment_method_data('credit_card_visa'); exp_year = card_data['expiration_year']  # Returns actual year like '2031'
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a critical test data file for payment method testing across multiple payment types and card networks. The file provides complete test credentials for 11 different payment methods covering credit cards (5 variants), direct debit (2 accounts), PayPal (2 accounts), Google Pay (1 account), and prepaid-only (empty placeholder). The credit card test numbers are standard test card numbers used in payment gateway testing - these are recognized by payment processors as test cards that will not process real transactions. The expiration_year field uses a special '+6' notation that is dynamically calculated at runtime by the get_payment_method_data() function to always provide a valid future expiration date. The 3ds_auth_result field indicates the expected outcome of 3D Secure authentication, which is important for testing secure payment flows. Direct debit uses German IBAN format (DE prefix) indicating this is primarily for European direct debit testing. PayPal accounts use yopmail.com disposable email addresses for testing. The prepaid_only entry is an empty object used when testing prepaid offers that don't require a payment method. This data is consumed by payment page objects and enrollment flows to fill payment forms during automated testing. The get_payment_method_data() function in test_flows_common.py loads this file and processes the dynamic expiration year calculation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test data file containing printer SKU profiles organized by printer model codename and geographic region. Each printer profile includes SKU numbers, easy enrollment capability flags, and supply variant types (IIC or IPH) for different regions (NA, EU, APJ). Used by test flows to select appropriate printer SKUs for virtual printer creation and enrollment testing based on target region and printer model, ensuring tests use valid SKU/region combinations that match production printer configurations.

data_structure.description: Dictionary mapping printer model codenames to SKU configuration objects

printer_models.note: NA has duplicate SKU with different easy_enroll values
SKU 1K7K7A appears in both NA and EU

supply_variants.description: Printer uses separate cartridges for each color (K, C, M, Y)
Printer uses integrated print head cartridges (CMY, K)

sku_format.structure: Typically starts with 1-3 digits followed by alphanumeric characters and ends with region letter (A=NA, B=EU, D=APJ)

field_definitions.description: HP printer SKU number (Stock Keeping Unit)
Whether this SKU supports easy enrollment flow (simplified onboarding)
Supply variant type - IIC (Individual Ink Cartridges) or IPH (Integrated Print Head)

usage_examples: from core.utils import load_json_file; printer_profiles = load_json_file('test_flows_common/printer_profiles.json'); manhattan_skus = printer_profiles['manhattan_yeti']['skus']
# Get all SKUs for a region: eu_skus = [sku_obj['sku'] for model in printer_profiles.values() for sku_obj in model['skus'].get('EU', [])]
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test data file provides printer SKU configurations for 14 different printer models identified by internal HP codenames (manhattan_yeti, vasari_yeti, novelli, etc.). Each printer model has SKU variants for different geographic regions (NA, EU, APJ) with region-specific SKU numbers. The SKU numbering follows a pattern where the last letter typically indicates the region (A for NA, B for EU, D for APJ). The easy_enroll flag indicates whether a SKU supports simplified enrollment flow - this is important for testing different enrollment paths. The supplyvariant field distinguishes between two ink cartridge technologies: IIC (Individual Ink Cartridges with separate K, C, M, Y cartridges) and IPH (Integrated Print Head with CMY and K cartridges). This distinction is critical because it affects supply level reporting, cartridge shipment logic, and RTP (Real-Time Printing) device status payloads. Some models like novelli have duplicate SKUs in the same region with different easy_enroll values, suggesting the same hardware can be configured differently. The malbec_yeti model has SKU 1K7K7A appearing in both NA and EU regions. This data is consumed by the create_virtual_printer() function in test_flows_common.py which uses the profile and derivative_model parameters to select the appropriate SKU for virtual printer creation. The supplyvariant value is used by send_rtp_devicestatus() to select the correct RTP payload template file (RTPDS-PayLoad-IIC.json or RTPDS-PayLoad-IPH.json). The printer_colors() function also uses supplyvariant to determine the correct color array (CMY/K for IPH or K/C/M/Y for IIC).

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive test flow helper module providing common utilities, API helpers, authentication functions, printer simulation functions, and test data management for the Instant Ink test automation framework. Contains functions for HPID account creation, OAuth token management, virtual printer creation and claiming, payment method handling, offer code generation, subscription state validation, email verification code retrieval, and various test flow orchestration utilities. Serves as the central utility library for complex multi-step test flows involving user authentication, printer enrollment, payment processing, and subscription management across multiple environments (pie, stage, production).

imports.import_statement: from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_fixed

classes.class_purpose: Named tuple class for storing virtual printer data returned from printer creation operations. Extends tuple to provide both indexed access and named attribute access to printer information including entity_id (serial number), model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard (claim code), and fingerprint. Used as the return type for create_virtual_printer() function to encapsulate all printer identification data needed for claiming and enrollment operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: printer = PrinterData(entity_id='ABC123', model_number='1G5L3A', device_uuid='uuid-string', cloud_id='cloud-id-string', postcard='postcard-base64', fingerprint='fingerprint-base64')

functions.method_signature: def __new__(cls, entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint)
functions.what_it_does: Creates a new PrinterData tuple instance by calling the parent tuple class __new__ method with all six printer data fields as a tuple. This allows PrinterData to be used as an immutable tuple while also providing named attribute access through the __init__ method.

functions.method_signature: def __init__(self, entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint)
functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrinterData instance by setting named attributes for all six printer data fields. Assigns self.entity_id, self.model_number, self.device_uuid, self.cloud_id, self.postcard, and self.fingerprint from the provided parameters. This allows accessing printer data via named attributes (printer.entity_id) in addition to tuple indexing (printer[0]).

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Dictionary containing configuration parameters for stack, profile, country, payment settings, and other test execution parameters

functions.what_it_does: Initializes all module-level global variables from the provided args dictionary and STACK_CONFIG. Extracts stack, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, tenant_country, tenant_country_short, tenant_country_language_code, tenant_address, headless, target, simulator_platform, target_width, target_height, supplyvariant, card_payment_gateway, and payment_method from args. Looks up the stack configuration from STACK_CONFIG dictionary using the stack value. Sets all URL endpoints (instantink_url, agena_url, cartridgepacker_url, fulfillmentservice_url, oss_url, hpid_url, stratus_authz, stratus_tropos, usermgt, ucde_authz, ucde_tropos, ucde_server, portalshell_url, onboarding_center_url) from the stack config. Sets Rails admin configuration (gemini_rails_admin_login_url, gemini_fetch_tenant_url, agena_cps_and_fulfillment_rails_admin_login_url, rails_admin_user, rails_admin_password). Sets client IDs and secrets (stratus_client_id, ucde_client_id, gemini_client_id, client_secrets). Sets MongoDB and S3 storage configuration. Logs all configuration values at debug level. This function must be called before any other test flow functions to ensure all global configuration is properly initialized.

Convenience setup function that calls set_globals with configuration values extracted from GlobalState. Builds the args dictionary by reading stack, printer_profile, derivative_model, biz_model, supplyvariant, country, country_code, language_code, address_payload, headless, target, simulator_platform, card_payment_gateway, and payment_method from GlobalState attributes. Then calls set_globals(args) to initialize all module-level globals. Also creates a 'temp' directory if it doesn't exist for temporary file storage. This function is typically called at the beginning of test execution to initialize the test environment from GlobalState configuration.
functions.usage_example: setup()  # Called at test initialization to set up environment from GlobalState

functions.what_it_does: Generates a unique test email address for tenant account creation using the Gmail plus addressing feature. Creates a random 8-character suffix using lowercase letters and digits. Constructs email in format 'hello.instantink+{suffix}@gmail.com'. Stores the generated email in the global _tenant_email variable. Logs the generated email at info level. Returns the generated email string. This email format allows all test emails to be received by a single Gmail account (hello.instantink@gmail.com) while maintaining unique addresses for each test run, which is useful for verification code retrieval and account isolation.

Authenticates with Gmail API using OAuth2 refresh token credentials. Creates a Credentials object with the GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN, GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET, and GMAIL_SCOPE from environment variables. Calls creds.refresh(Request()) to obtain a fresh access token from the refresh token. If refresh fails with invalid_grant or invalid_request error, raises RuntimeError with message to update the refresh token. Builds and returns a Gmail API service object using googleapiclient.discovery.build with the refreshed credentials. This service object is used by fetch_verification_code to retrieve verification emails.

Extracts the 6-digit verification code from HPID verification email HTML content. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse the HTML. Searches for a

element with class 'code' which contains the verification code. If the element is not found, raises RuntimeError. Extracts the text content from the element using get_text(strip=True). Logs the extracted code at debug level. Returns the verification code string. This function is deprecated in favor of new_extract_verification_code which has more robust pattern matching.

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address to search for verification code

functions.what_it_does: Fetches the verification code from Gmail for the specified tenant email address with automatic retry logic. Decorated with @retry to attempt up to 3 times with 5-second wait between attempts. Calls authenticate_gmail() to get Gmail API service. Constructs query string 'to:{tenant_email} newer_than:5m' to find recent messages. Calls Gmail API users().messages().list() to search for messages. If no messages found, raises RuntimeError. Gets the full message content using users().messages().get() with format='full' for the first message. Extracts the message parts and searches for HTML body data in either the main payload body or in parts with mimeType='text/html'. If no HTML data found, raises RuntimeError. Base64 decodes the HTML data. Calls new_extract_verification_code() to extract the code from HTML. Returns the verification code string. The retry decorator ensures transient failures (email not yet received) are automatically retried.
functions.usage_example: code = fetch_verification_code('hello.instantink+test123@gmail.com')  # Automatically retries up to 3 times

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute during account creation

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to use, defaults to global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new HPID account through the HPID sign-up UI flow. Navigates to the HPID URL (_hpid_url). Sets viewport size to 1366x768. Waits for and clicks the sign-up button. Fills the firstName field with DEFAULT_FIRSTNAME. Fills the lastName field with DEFAULT_LASTNAME. Fills the email field with the provided email or global _tenant_email. Fills the password field with DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks the sign-up submit button. Waits for page load. Executes the optional callback function if provided. Enters a 60-second polling loop checking for either the submit-code element (onboarding center flow) or 'myaccount' in URL (my account flow). If submit-code element appears, calls confirm_code_by_onboarding_center(). If myaccount URL detected, calls confirm_code_by_my_account(). Sleeps 2 seconds between checks. Logs success message. Returns the page object. This function handles the HPID account creation flow which can branch into different verification flows depending on the environment.

parameters.name: check_func
parameters.description: Lambda or function to execute safely

functions.what_it_does: Safely executes a check function and returns its result, catching any exceptions and returning False if an exception occurs. Wraps the check_func call in a try-except block. If the function executes successfully, returns its result. If any exception occurs, catches it and returns False. This is used in polling loops to check for element presence or URL conditions without raising exceptions when elements are not yet present.

Confirms HPID account creation by entering verification code in the My Account flow. Clicks the security tab using locator 'li[id="security"]'. Clicks the Verify button using get_by_role. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the code from email. Fills the verification code textbox using get_by_role with the retrieved code. Clicks the Verify button again to submit the code. This handles the verification flow when HPID creation redirects to the My Account page.

Confirms HPID account creation by entering verification code in the Onboarding Center flow. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the code from email. Fills the code field (#code) with the retrieved code. Clicks the submit-code button (#submit-code). Attempts to wait for country residence selector (#countryResidence) with 20-second timeout. If country selector appears, clicks it and selects the country using data-value attribute matching _tenant_country_short. Clicks the continue button (#continue-button). If country selector does not appear (timeout exception), logs debug message and continues. Logs success message. This handles the verification flow when HPID creation goes through the Onboarding Center which may require country selection.

Retrieves the verification code from Gmail with retry logic. Initializes code variable to None. Loops 3 attempts (1 to 3). In each attempt, tries to call fetch_verification_code(_tenant_email). If successful, breaks out of loop. If exception occurs, logs warning with attempt number and error message, then sleeps 15 seconds before next attempt. After loop, if code is still None, raises RuntimeError indicating all 3 attempts failed. Logs the retrieved code at info level. Returns the code string. This provides a higher-level retry wrapper around fetch_verification_code with longer delays between attempts.
functions.usage_example: code = get_verification_code()  # Retries up to 3 times with 15-second delays

parameters.name: test_requirements
parameters.description: Dictionary containing test requirements including optional plan_pages key

functions.docstring: Determine plan pages from requirements or use default.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan page count from test requirements or returns default value. Extracts plan_pages value from test_requirements dictionary using get(). If plan_pages is not present or is None/empty, sets plan_pages to default value '100' and logs info message. If plan_pages is present, logs info message with the value from requirements. Returns the plan_pages value. This allows tests to specify custom plan page counts or fall back to a sensible default.

parameters.name: plan_type_param
parameters.description: Plan type parameter value, typically 'ink_only' or other

parameters.name: test_requirements
parameters.description: Dictionary containing test requirements including optional paper_option key

functions.docstring: Determine plan type from parameter or requirements.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan type (ink_only or ink_and_paper) from parameter or test requirements. If plan_type_param is not 'ink_only', logs info message and returns plan_type_param as-is. If plan_type_param is 'ink_only', extracts paper_option from test_requirements. Sets plan_type to 'ink_and_paper' if paper_option is 'add', otherwise sets to 'ink_only'. Logs info message with determined plan type and paper_option value. Returns the plan_type string. This allows plan type to be determined either explicitly via parameter or implicitly via paper_option requirement.

Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account through the complete sign-up flow. First tests browser connectivity by navigating to about:blank. Then navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait and 120-second timeout. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Attempts to accept privacy banner by clicking #onetrust-accept-btn-handler with 10-second timeout, logs debug if not found. Locates sign-up button using multiple test-id selectors and waits for visibility with 3000-second timeout (likely a typo, should be 30 seconds). Clicks sign-up button. Waits for create-account button to be visible. Clicks create-account button. Waits for sign-in element to be visible. Waits explicitly for firstName field to be visible with 60-second timeout. Waits additional 1 second for form to be interactive. Fills firstName, lastName, email (_tenant_email), and password (DEFAULT_PASSWORD) fields. Clicks market checkbox. Clicks sign-up submit button. Waits for submit-code element with 30-second timeout. Attempts to fetch verification code 3 times with 5-second delays between attempts. If all attempts fail, raises RuntimeError. Fills code field with retrieved code. Clicks submit-code button. Waits for stepper element to be visible with 120-second timeout. Logs success message. Navigates to _portalshell_url with 60-second timeout. Waits 60 seconds. Attempts to click side menu toggle, ignores exception if not found. Clicks HP Instant Ink side menu item with 120-second timeout. Returns the page object. This is a comprehensive account creation flow that handles the full v2 enrollment UI including privacy banner, account creation form, email verification, and navigation to portal shell.

parameters.name: oobe
parameters.description: Whether this is an OOBE (out-of-box experience) login flow

functions.what_it_does: Logs into an existing Instant Ink v2 account. Logs info message with username. Locates sign-up button using multiple test-id selectors. Checks if sign-up button is visible with 5-second timeout. If not visible, navigates to _instantink_url with 120-second timeout. Attempts to accept privacy banner by clicking #onetrust-accept-btn-handler with 10-second timeout, logs debug if not found. Clicks the first sign-up button. Sleeps 5 seconds. Waits for and clicks sign-in button. Fills username field with provided username. Clicks 'Use password' link. Fills password field with DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks sign-in button. If oobe parameter is True, waits for 'Add a different printer' radio button to be visible with 180-second timeout, clicks it, waits for continue button to be visible with 8-second timeout, and clicks continue button. Returns the page object. This handles the login flow for existing accounts with optional OOBE device selection flow.

Logs out of Instant Ink v2 account. Locates account button using test-id 'account-button'. Locates sign-out button using test-id 'sign-out-button'. Uses expect() to verify account button is visible with 3-second timeout. Clicks account button. Uses expect() to verify sign-out button is visible with 3-second timeout. Clicks sign-out button. Locates sign-up button using multiple test-id selectors. Uses expect() to verify sign-up button is visible with 120-second timeout to confirm logout completed. This ensures the user is fully logged out and returned to the landing page.

Onboards an HPID account to the UCDE (Unified Cloud Device Ecosystem) system via OAuth flow and API calls. Generates a random GUID for OAuth state parameter. Constructs UCDE authorization URL with openid/email/profile scope, authorization code response type, state GUID, UCDE client ID, and redirect URI. Logs the authorization URL at debug level. Navigates page to the authorization URL with 60-second timeout. Waits for page load. Sleeps 5 seconds. Captures the final URL after OAuth redirect. Logs the final URL. Verifies 'code=' is present in URL, raises RuntimeError if not found. Extracts the authorization code from URL query parameters. Logs the retrieved OAuth code. Constructs token exchange URL. Creates Basic authentication header by base64 encoding client_id:client_secret. Sends POST request to token endpoint with authorization_code grant type, code, and redirect_uri. Logs token exchange response. Raises exception if response status is not successful. Extracts access_token and id_token from response JSON. Raises RuntimeError if either token is missing. Logs success message. Constructs UCDE account creation URL. Sends POST request with Bearer access_token, JSON body containing countrySet array with _tenant_country_short, idToken, and language code. Logs account creation response. Raises exception if response status is not 201. Logs success message with stack and country/language. Returns the page object. This function performs the complete HPID to UCDE onboarding flow including OAuth authorization, token exchange, and account creation API calls.

Obtains an OAuth access token for the Gemini client using client credentials flow. Constructs token URL using _stratus_authz endpoint with /openid/v1/token path. Creates Basic authentication header by base64 encoding gemini_client_id:gemini_client_secret. Sends POST request to token endpoint with Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded, Authorization Basic header, and grant_type=client_credentials in body. Raises exception if response status is not successful. Extracts access_token from response JSON. Returns the access token string. This token is used for Gemini API calls that require service-level authentication rather than user-level authentication.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to get token for, defaults to global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Obtains an organization-aware user access token through a multi-step token exchange process. Step 1: Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get Gemini service token, logs first 8 characters. Step 2: Sends GET request to usermgt service to find user by email using Gemini token, extracts userResourceId and idpId from first result, logs values. Step 3: Sends GET request to usermgt service to get user tenant details using userResourceId, extracts tenantResourceId from first result, logs value. Step 4: Gets Stratus client token by sending POST to token endpoint with client_credentials grant using Stratus client ID and secret, logs first 8 characters. Step 5: Performs token exchange by sending POST to token endpoint with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange, subject_token=Stratus client token, subject_token_type=access_token, requested_user_type=stratusid, requested_user=userResourceId, and tenant_id=tenantResourceId. Extracts org-aware access token from response. Logs first 8 characters of final token. Returns tuple of (org_token, tenant_id). This complex token exchange flow is required to obtain a user-scoped token that is aware of the user's organization/tenant context, which is needed for device claiming and other tenant-scoped operations.

functions.method_signature: def retry_operation(operation, operation_name='Operation', max_attempts=5, delay=2, on_retry=None)

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Callable function to execute with retry logic

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Delay in seconds between retry attempts

parameters.name: on_retry
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute before each retry

functions.what_it_does: Generic retry wrapper that executes an operation with configurable retry logic. Loops from attempt 1 to max_attempts. Logs info message with operation name and attempt number. Executes the operation callable. If successful, logs success message and returns the result. If exception occurs, logs warning with error message. If this was the last attempt, logs error message and re-raises the exception. If on_retry callback is provided, executes it in a try-except block, logging warning if callback fails. Logs info message about retrying with delay. Sleeps for the specified delay. Continues to next attempt. This provides a reusable retry pattern for operations that may fail transiently, with optional cleanup/recovery logic via the on_retry callback.
functions.usage_example: result = retry_operation(lambda: api_call(), operation_name='API Call', max_attempts=3, delay=5, on_retry=lambda: reset_connection())

functions.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer through the G2 simulator API with retry logic for each step. Defines helper function make_request(method, url, **kwargs) that sends HTTP request using session, raises exception on error, and returns response. Step 1 (step_create_printer): Constructs URL to G2 simulator printers endpoint. Builds JSON body with stack, profile, biz_model, offer=1, fipsflag='true', and derivative_model. Logs body. Calls make_request with POST method. Returns response JSON. Wraps step 1 in retry_operation with 5 max attempts. Extracts entity_id, model_number, and device_uuid from response. Logs printer creation with serial number, model, and UUID. Step 2 (step_register_printer): Constructs URL to register endpoint for entity_id. Calls make_request with POST method. Returns cloud_id from response JSON. Wraps step 2 in retry_operation with 5 max attempts. Logs cloud_id. Step 3 (step_get_postcard): Constructs URL to claimpostcard endpoint for entity_id. Calls make_request with POST method. Gets response text and strips whitespace. If text starts with '{', base64 encodes it, otherwise removes quotes. Returns postcard string. Wraps step 3 in retry_operation with 3 max attempts. Step 4 (step_get_fingerprint): Constructs URL to devicefingerprint endpoint for entity_id. Calls make_request with GET method. Gets response text and strips whitespace. If text starts with '{', base64 encodes it, otherwise removes quotes. Returns fingerprint string. Wraps step 4 in retry_operation with 3 max attempts. Creates PrinterData tuple with all six values. Appends to global _printer_created list. Returns PrinterData object. This function orchestrates the complete virtual printer creation flow with robust retry logic for each API call.
functions.usage_example: printer = create_virtual_printer(); entity_id = printer.entity_id; cloud_id = printer.cloud_id

functions.method_signature: def claim_virtual_printer(org_token, tenant_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, fingerprint)
functions.what_it_does: Claims a virtual printer to a tenant account through the UCDE API with retry logic. Logs info message with model number and tenant ID. Constructs autoClaimRequest payload with productNumber, uuid, claimPostcard, fingerprint, and selectedBizModel (_biz_model). Logs truncated payload at debug level. Loops up to 10 attempts. Logs attempt number. Sends POST request to UCDE tropos devices endpoint for the tenant with JSON payload and Bearer token authorization. Logs response status and text at debug level. Raises exception if status is not successful. Logs success message and returns (exits function). If exception occurs, logs warning with attempt number and error. If this was the last attempt, logs error and re-raises exception. Logs retry message and sleeps 2 seconds before next attempt. This function handles the device claiming API call which associates a virtual printer with a user's tenant account, with robust retry logic to handle transient failures.

Adds a shipping address to a tenant account via the Gemini comfe API. Logs info message with tenant ID. Checks if _tenant_address is a list, if so extracts first element, otherwise uses it directly. Creates address_payload dictionary by filtering out any keys starting with 'fullState' from address_data. Logs address payload at debug level. Sends POST request to instantink comfe shipping addresses endpoint with tenantId query parameter, JSON payload, and Bearer token authorization. Logs response status and text at debug level. If response status is not 201, raises RuntimeError with status code. Logs success message. This function adds the shipping address required for subscription enrollment and cartridge shipments.

Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org token and tenant ID, logs success. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the returned PrinterData tuple into entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint. Logs printer details with truncated postcard and fingerprint (first 8 chars). Calls claim_virtual_printer() with all required parameters. Logs success message. Sleeps 600 seconds (10 minutes) as temporary fix for printer availability issues due to known bugs III-2131 / IIGAR-2774. Returns the PrinterData object. This is a high-level convenience function that combines printer creation and claiming into a single operation with a workaround delay for backend synchronization issues.

Orchestrates the complete flow of creating and claiming a virtual printer plus adding shipping address. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org token and tenant ID, logs success. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the returned PrinterData tuple into entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, fingerprint. Logs printer details with truncated postcard and fingerprint. Calls claim_virtual_printer() with all required parameters. Logs printer claimed success. Calls add_address_to_tenant() with org token and tenant ID. Logs address added success. Logs info message about 10-minute wait for printer availability. Sleeps 600 seconds as temporary fix for backend synchronization issues. Returns the PrinterData object. This is the most complete setup function that prepares everything needed for enrollment testing including printer and address.

parameters.name: payment_type
parameters.description: Payment method identifier, defaults to global _payment_method or 'credit_card_master'

functions.what_it_does: Loads payment method test data from JSON file and processes dynamic expiration year. If payment_type is None, uses global _payment_method or defaults to 'credit_card_master'. Constructs path to payment_method_data.json file in same directory. Calls load_json_file() to load the JSON data. Checks if payment_type exists in data, if not raises ValueError with available types list. Creates a copy of the payment data for the specified type. Checks if 'expiration_year' key exists in payment data. If expiration_year value is a string starting with '+', extracts the numeric offset, gets current year from datetime, adds the offset, and replaces expiration_year with the calculated year as a string. Returns the processed payment data dictionary. This function provides dynamic expiration year calculation so test credit cards always have valid future expiration dates.

Creates an OOBE (out-of-box experience) registration record in Agena for a printer. Constructs payload dictionary with model_number, serial_number (entity_id), country (_tenant_country_short), and empty strings for sku, ink_declaration, supply_types, supply_levels, supply_states, unenrolled_pages, ui_phase, enrollment_kit, jump_id, and ip_address. Constructs URL to Agena oobe_registrations endpoint. Creates headers with Version HTTP/1.0, Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=2, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL and payload. Sends POST request with headers and JSON payload. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Returns the response object. This creates the initial OOBE registration record that tracks the printer setup flow.

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of card header to find

functions.what_it_does: Finds a card body element by locating its header text. Locates elements with class 'card-header' that have exact text match using :text-is() selector. Asserts that at least one matching header is found, raises assertion error with message if not found. Navigates up one level from header using '..' locator. Locates the '.card-body' element within that parent. Returns the card body locator. This is a helper for extracting information from card-based UI layouts commonly used in admin interfaces.

Extracts text content from a card body by finding its header. Calls find_card_body_by_header() to locate the card body. Calls inner_text() on the card body locator to get text content. Strips whitespace from the text. Returns the cleaned text string. This is useful for extracting field values from card-based admin UI layouts.

Extracts a list of link URLs from a card body by finding its header. Calls find_card_body_by_header() to locate the card body. Locates all 'a' elements within the card body. Iterates through all link elements using .all(). For each link, gets the 'href' attribute using get_attribute(). Returns a list of href values. This is useful for extracting multiple links from card-based admin UI sections.

Extracts the last numeric value from the current page URL. Gets the current URL from page.url. Uses re.findall() with pattern r'\d+' to find all sequences of digits in the URL. Returns the last number from the matches list if any numbers found, otherwise returns None. This is useful for extracting IDs or other numeric identifiers from URLs like '/subscriptions/12345/details'.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text string to extract numbers from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts all numeric values (integers and decimals) from a text string. Returns empty list if text is None or empty. Strips whitespace from text. Uses re.findall() with pattern r'\d+\.\d+|\d+' to match decimal numbers (digits.digits) or integers (digits). Returns the list of matched number strings if any found, otherwise returns empty list. This is useful for parsing numeric values from UI text that may contain mixed content.

Sends an RTP (Real-Time Printing) device status message to the G2 simulator for a virtual printer. This is an extremely complex function that constructs and sends printer telemetry data. Initializes counter values: bizmodelFlag=1409286144, lastValidConfigSeqNum=1, lastReceivedConfigSeqNum=1, lastValidCredentialSeqNum=1, supplyInsertionCount=1, totalImpressionCount=0, bizlogicImpressionCount=0, internalReportImpressionCount=0, snapshotCountersSum=0. Gets current UTC timestamp in ISO format. Defines crsSessionData_template as base64-encoded binary template. Defines field_map dictionary mapping field names to (offset, length, format) tuples for binary packing. Creates mutable bytearray from decoded template. Defines set_field() helper that packs values into the bytearray at specified offsets using struct.pack() for numeric fields or ASCII encoding for string fields. Calls set_field() to update totalImpressionCount, bizlogicImpressionCount, internalReportImpressionCount, snapshotCountersSum, and manufacturingDeviceUuid in the binary data. Base64 encodes the modified bytearray to create crsSessionData string. Defines decode_crssession() helper that unpacks the binary data to verify values. Calls decode_crssession() and logs the decoded values at debug level. Logs truncated crsSessionData at debug level. Creates replacements dictionary with all placeholder values including cloud_id, uuid, crsSessionData, counter values, timestamps, etc. Logs replacements at debug level. Constructs path to RTPDS-PayLoad-{_supplyvariant}.json template file based on supply variant (IIC or IPH). Raises FileNotFoundError if template doesn't exist. Reads template file content. Performs string replacement for all placeholders in template using {{key}} format. Parses the replaced template as JSON. Defines numeric_keys set containing field names that should be integers. Defines coerce_numerics() recursive function that converts string values to integers for numeric fields. Calls coerce_numerics() on the payload to ensure proper types. Logs the complete payload at debug level. Constructs URL to G2 simulator rtpdevicestatustriggers endpoint. Logs info message with entity_id and supplyvariant. Sends PUT request with JSON payload. Logs raw response at debug level. Logs response status and text at info level. Raises exception if response is not successful. Returns the response object. This function simulates printer telemetry reporting which triggers backend processing for supply levels, page counts, and subscription billing.

Generates an offer code from an offer identifier via Agena API. Calls get_org_aware_token() to get org token (only uses token, discards tenant_id). Constructs URL to Agena codes endpoint with identifier_code. Creates headers with Version HTTP/1.0, Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=1, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends POST request with headers. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Extracts code from posaCodes[0]['code'] path. Logs info message with generated code. Returns the code string. This converts an offer identifier into an actual offer code that can be used during enrollment.
functions.usage_example: code = offer_request('10232')  # Returns actual offer code like 'ABC123XYZ'

functions.method_signature: def get_offer(region: str, type: str = 'free', amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> dict

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for amount greater than this value

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for amount less than this value

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an offer from OFFERS_DATA that matches the specified criteria. Raises ValueError if region is not provided. Raises ValueError if region is not found in OFFERS_DATA. Gets the offers array for the region. Iterates through each offer in the array. Checks if offer type matches the specified type, skips if not. If no amount filters are provided, returns the first matching offer immediately. Gets amountCents from offer, skips if not present. Converts amount to integer, logs warning and skips if conversion fails. Checks amount filters: skips if amount_equal specified and amount doesn't match, skips if amount_greater specified and amount is not greater, skips if amount_less specified and amount is not less. If all filters pass, returns the offer. If loop completes without finding a match, builds error message listing which filters were applied. Raises ValueError with descriptive message. This function provides flexible offer selection with multiple filtering options for test data.
functions.usage_example: offer = get_offer('US', type='prepaid', amount_equal=6000); offer_guid = offer['guid']

functions.method_signature: def get_offer_code(offer_type: str = 'free', amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> str

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for amount greater than this value

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for amount less than this value

functions.what_it_does: Generates an offer code for the current test country based on offer criteria. Gets region from GlobalState.country_code. Raises ValueError if country_code is not set. Calls get_offer() with region, offer_type, and amount filters. Extracts identifier from the returned offer. Raises ValueError if identifier is not found in offer. Calls offer_request() with the identifier to generate the actual code. Logs info message with offer type, code, and region. Returns the generated code string. This is a high-level convenience function that combines offer selection and code generation.

Captures user information by intercepting the /v3/usermgtsvc/users/me API request from the dashboard. Logs info message about waiting for the request. Uses page.expect_response() context manager with URL pattern '**/v3/usermgtsvc/users/me' and 120-second timeout. Inside the context, reloads the page to trigger the API request. Captures the response from the context manager. Gets the request object from the response. Attempts to parse response as JSON, if parsing fails logs warning and creates error dict. Builds captured_data dictionary with 'request' section containing method, url, headers dict, and body (post_data), and 'response' section containing status, status_text, headers dict, and data (parsed JSON or error). Prints the captured data. Returns the captured_data dictionary. If any exception occurs, logs warning and returns None. This function intercepts network traffic to extract authentication tokens and user data from API responses.

Makes an API request to /api/users/me using token captured from dashboard. Calls user_info_from_dashboard() to capture user data and token. Prints the user data. Extracts authorization header from request headers and removes 'Bearer ' prefix to get token. Constructs URL to instantink /api/users/me endpoint. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/json, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL and headers. Sends GET request with headers. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data dictionary. This function uses the captured token to make additional API calls for user information.

Retrieves subscription data from Gemini API using service token. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get Gemini service token. Constructs URL to instantink /api/users/subscriptions/{id} endpoint. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/json, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends GET request with headers. Logs info message with response status and text. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data dictionary. This provides direct API access to subscription data using service-level authentication.

Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data. Calls subscription_data_from_gemini() with the subscription ID. Attempts to navigate the nested dictionary path userSubInfo -> subscriptionInfo -> minSubInfo -> subscriptionState. Returns the subscriptionState value if found. If KeyError occurs (path doesn't exist), returns None. This provides a safe way to extract subscription state with error handling.

Extracts subscription state from dashboard /api/users/me response. Calls request_me_by_dashboard() to get user data. Attempts to navigate the nested dictionary path userMinSubInfo -> minSubInfo[0] -> subscriptionState. Returns the subscriptionState value if found. If KeyError occurs, returns None. This provides subscription state extraction from the user-level API response.

Validates that subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds. Records start time. Enters loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. Calls get_subscription_state() with subscription ID. If state matches expected_state, logs info message and breaks out of loop. Sleeps 1 second before next check. After loop, asserts that state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError with descriptive message if not. This provides polling-based state validation with timeout.
functions.usage_example: validate_subscription_state('12345', 'ACTIVE')  # Polls until ACTIVE or 60 seconds

functions.what_it_does: Validates subscription state from dashboard API within 60 seconds. Records start time. Enters loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. Calls get_subscription_state_by_me_from_dashboard() with page. If state matches expected_state, logs info message and breaks out of loop. Sleeps 1 second before next check. After loop, asserts that state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError if not. This provides polling-based state validation using the dashboard API.

Removes web services from a virtual printer via G2 simulator API. Logs info message with entity_id. Constructs URL to G2 simulator webservices endpoint for the printer. Sends DELETE request. Logs debug message with response status and text. If response status is not 200, raises RuntimeError with status code. Logs success message. This simulates disabling web services on a printer, which can be used to test offline scenarios.

Enables web services on a virtual printer via G2 simulator API. Logs info message with entity_id. Constructs URL to G2 simulator webservices endpoint for the printer. Sends PUT request. Logs debug message with response status and text. If response status is not 200, raises RuntimeError with status code. Logs success message. This simulates enabling web services on a printer after they were disabled.

Returns the list of ink colors for the current printer based on supply variant. If global _supplyvariant is 'IPH', returns ['CMY', 'K'] (Integrated Print Head with combined color cartridge and separate black). If _supplyvariant is 'IIC', returns ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] (Individual Ink Cartridges with separate cartridges for each color). Returns the colors list. This is used to determine the correct color array for supply level reporting and RTP payloads.

Parses date strings in multiple formats into datetime objects. Defines list of date format strings including '%b %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM), '%Y/%m/%d %I:%M:%S %p' (2025/09/14 02:41:13 PM), '%d %b %Y, %H:%M:%S' (14 Sep 2025, 14:41:13), '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S' (14/09/2025 14:41:13), '%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p' (09/14/2025 2:41:11 PM), '%B %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (September 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM). Iterates through each format. Attempts to parse date_string using datetime.strptime() with the format. If successful, returns the datetime object. If ValueError occurs, continues to next format. If all formats fail, raises ValueError with the original date string. This provides robust date parsing for various international date formats.

Extracts and parses a date from text containing mixed content. Cleans text by replacing multiple whitespace with single space and stripping. Defines list of regex patterns to match various date formats. Iterates through each pattern. Uses re.search() to find pattern in cleaned text. If match found, extracts the date string from group 1. Logs info message with extracted date string. Calls parse_flexible_date() to parse the date string. Returns the datetime object. If no pattern matches, raises ValueError with the cleaned text. This combines pattern matching and date parsing to extract dates from UI text.

Retrieves all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API for the current country. Constructs URL to Agena plans endpoint with country=_tenant_country_short, program=i_ink, and obsolete=false query parameters. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get service token. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=3, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends GET request with headers. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data (array of plan objects). This retrieves the complete catalog of available plans for the test country.
functions.usage_example: plans = get_plans_data(); plan_100 = [p for p in plans if p['pages'] == 100][0]

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Calls get_plans_data() to get all plans. Converts value to integer. Filters plans array to find plans where plan[key] equals value. If no matching plans found, raises ValueError with key and value. Returns the list of matching plan objects. This provides filtered plan selection for test data.

Retrieves all yearly Instant Ink plans data from Agena API. Constructs URL to Agena plans endpoint with country=_tenant_country_short, program=i_yearly_ink, and obsolete=false. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get service token. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=3, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends GET request with headers. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data. This retrieves yearly subscription plans which have different pricing and page allotments.

Retrieves yearly plans filtered by attribute value. Calls get_yearly_plans_data() to get all yearly plans. Converts value to integer. Filters plans array to find plans where plan[key] equals value. If no matching plans found, raises ValueError. Returns the list of matching plan objects. This provides filtered yearly plan selection.

Returns the expected billing status code based on payment method and gateway. Initializes status to None. If 'credit_card' in _payment_method, sets status to 'CPT-100'. If _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides status to 'STRATUS-PAYMENT-COMPLETE'. If 'direct_debit' in _payment_method, sets status to 'DIRECT-DEBIT-SUCCESS'. If 'paypal' in _payment_method, sets status to 'PAY-PAL-SUCCESS'. If 'prepaid' in _payment_method, sets status to 'PEGASUS-SUCCESS'. Returns the status string. This provides the expected billing status code for validation based on the payment method configuration.
functions.usage_example: expected_status = billing_status_code()  # Returns 'CPT-100' for credit card with PGS

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Whether this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund event response status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines refund_status_code dictionary mapping payment method types to full/partial refund status codes. For credit_card: full='PAYMENT-GATEWAY-REFUNDED', partial='PAYMENT-GATEWAY-PARTIAL-REFUNDED'. For direct_debit: full='DIRECT-DEBIT-REFUND-SUCCESS', partial='DIRECT-DEBIT-PARTIAL-REFUND-SUCCESS'. For paypal: full='PAY-PAL-REFUNDED', partial='PAY-PAL-REFUND-PARTIAL'. For prepaid_only: full='PEGASUS-REFUNDED', partial='PEGASUS-PARTIAL-REFUNDED'. Normalizes _payment_method to base type (credit_card, direct_debit, paypal, prepaid_only). Gets the refund codes for the payment method. Returns full or partial code based on full parameter. This provides expected refund event status codes for validation.

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Whether this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines refund_status_code dictionary mapping payment method types to full/partial refund statuses. For credit_card: full='FULL-REFUND', partial='PARTIAL-REFUND'. For direct_debit: full='DIRECT-DEBIT-REFUND', partial='PARTIAL-REFUNDED'. For paypal: full='PAY-PAL-REFUND', partial='PARTIAL-REFUND'. For prepaid_only: full='PEGASUS-REFUNDED', partial='PARTIAL-REFUND'. Normalizes _payment_method to base type. Gets the refund codes for the payment method. Returns full or partial code based on full parameter. This provides expected refund status codes for validation.

Returns the payment engine name based on payment method and gateway. Initializes payment_engine to None. If 'credit_card' in _payment_method, sets to 'payment_gateway'. If _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides to 'stratus'. If 'direct_debit' in _payment_method, sets to 'pgs_direct_debit'. If 'paypal' in _payment_method, sets to 'pgs_pay_pal'. If 'prepaid_only' in _payment_method, sets to 'pegasus'. Returns the payment_engine string. This identifies which payment processing system is used for the current payment method.
functions.usage_example: engine = payment_engine()  # Returns 'payment_gateway' or 'stratus' for credit cards

functions.what_it_does: Returns a list of countries that do not require state/province selection in address forms. Returns hardcoded list containing Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Luxembourg, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Singapore, Sweden, Switzerland. This is used to determine whether state field should be required in address validation.

Returns a list of countries that support direct debit payment method. Returns hardcoded list containing Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Spain. This is used to determine whether direct debit payment option should be available for the test country.

Checks if an element is actionable by verifying it is the topmost element at its center point. Wraps logic in try-except to return False on any exception. Gets bounding box of the locator. Returns False if bounding box is None (element not visible). Calculates center point coordinates: center_x = x + width/2, center_y = y + height/2. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that calls document.elementFromPoint(x, y) to get the topmost element at the center point. Checks if the element at that point is either the target element itself or contains the target element. Returns the boolean result. This is useful for checking if an element is actually clickable or if it's obscured by another element.

Parses a JSON string into a Python object with error handling. Wraps json.loads() in try-except. If parsing succeeds, returns the parsed object. If exception occurs, logs error message with exception details and re-raises the exception. This provides JSON parsing with logging for debugging.

Retrieves printer SKU data from Agena API for the current country and derivative model. Constructs URL to Agena printer_skus endpoint with _tenant_country_short and _derivative_model path parameters. Calls get_token_gemini_client() to get service token. Creates headers with Authorization Bearer token, Accept application/vnd.agena+json;version=3, and Content-Type application/json. Logs debug message with URL. Sends GET request with headers. Raises exception if response is not successful. Parses response JSON. Returns the response data. This retrieves printer configuration data for the specified model and country.

Extracts CSS styling details for plan card elements. Calls element.evaluate() with JavaScript function that gets computed styles using window.getComputedStyle(). Extracts border-radius and margin CSS properties. Returns dictionary with 'border-radius' and 'margin' keys. This is used for visual validation of plan card styling.

Extracts comprehensive font and text styling details from an element. Calls element.evaluate() with JavaScript function that gets computed styles. Extracts font-family, font-size, font-weight, font-style, line-height, letter-spacing, text-decoration, text-transform, color, font-variant, text-align, background-color, and fill CSS properties. Returns dictionary with all extracted style properties. This is used for detailed typography and styling validation.
functions.usage_example: font_details = get_font_details(heading_element); assert font_details['font-family'] == 'Forma DJR UI'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific CSS property has the expected value. Calls get_font_details() to get all font details. Asserts that font_details[property] equals value, raising AssertionError with descriptive message if not. Returns the actual property value. This provides assertion-based style validation.

Normalizes font family values for comparison by extracting the first font name. Returns value unchanged if it's None or empty. Splits value by comma to handle font stacks. Takes the first font name. Removes double quotes and single quotes. Strips whitespace. Returns the normalized font name. This handles font family values like '"Forma DJR UI", Arial, sans-serif' and returns just 'Forma DJR UI'.
functions.usage_example: normalized = _normalize_font_family('"Forma DJR UI", Arial')  # Returns 'Forma DJR UI'

parameters.name: landing_page
parameters.description: Landing page object with footer element attributes

functions.what_it_does: Validates footer element fonts and styling against expected values. Defines checks list with tuples of (attribute_name, expected_styles_dict, label). For each check: gets element from landing_page using getattr, asserts element is not None, calls get_font_details() to get actual styles, iterates through expected properties, normalizes font-family values using _normalize_font_family(), asserts actual equals expected with descriptive error message, logs success message. Checks include footer_section background color, footer_hp_link font, footer_privacy_statement font, footer_terms_of_use font, footer_cookies font, footer_privacy_choice font, footer_iitos font, and footer_copyright font. All footer links expect Forma DJR UI font family, 16px size, and 20px or 24px line height. This provides comprehensive footer styling validation.

parameters.name: new_account_auth_option
parameters.description: Authentication option for new accounts - 'use_password' or 'send_sign_in_code'

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account with support for both password and OTP authentication flows. This is an enhanced version of create_ii_v2_account with more robust handling of different authentication paths. Tests browser connectivity with about:blank. Navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Accepts privacy banner if present. Locates and clicks sign-up button. Clicks create-account button. Checks if password screen appears first (sometimes happens), if so navigates back to email screen using back button. Fills email field with _tenant_email. Clicks next button. Detects whether this is new email flow (firstName field appears) or existing email flow. For new email flow: fills firstName and lastName, validates new_account_auth_option parameter, if 'send_sign_in_code' clicks that button, if 'use_password' clicks use password button and fills password then clicks create button, waits for OTP code field, fetches verification code with 3 retries, fills code and submits, waits for stepper element. For existing email flow: clicks use password button, fills password, clicks sign-in button, waits 5 seconds for stabilization. Navigates to portalshell URL. Waits 60 seconds. Clicks side menu toggle if present. Clicks HP Instant Ink menu item. Returns page object. This function handles multiple authentication flows including new account with password, new account with OTP, and existing account login.

Extracts 6-digit verification code from email HTML using multiple fallback strategies. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse HTML. First tries to find

element with class 'code' and returns its text if found. If not found, joins all stripped text strings from the HTML. Tries multiple regex patterns in order: 'one-time passcode (OTP): (\d{6})', 'one-time passcode.*?\b(\d{6})\b', 'The OTP will expire.*?\b(\d{6})\b', '\b(\d{6})\b' (any 6-digit number). For each pattern, uses re.search() with DOTALL flag. If match found, returns the captured 6-digit code. If no pattern matches, raises RuntimeError. This is more robust than extract_verification_code as it handles multiple email formats and has fallback patterns.

variables_and_constants.constant_value: Dictionary with gemini_rails_admin_endpoint, agena_cps_and_fulfillment_rails_admin_endpoint, fetch_tenant_endpoint, rails_admin_user, rails_admin_password
Large nested dictionary with configuration for pie, stage, and production stacks
requests.Session() with verify=False and proxy configuration

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the largest and most complex file in the batch - a comprehensive helper module containing 60+ functions for test flow orchestration. The file serves as the central utility library for the Instant Ink test automation framework, providing functions for: (1) Environment configuration and global state management via set_globals() and setup(). (2) HPID account creation and authentication via create_hpid(), create_ii_v2_account(), create_new_ii_v2_account(), login_ii_v2_account(), logout_ii_v2_account(). (3) OAuth token management and multi-step token exchange via authenticate_gmail(), get_token_gemini_client(), get_org_aware_token(), onboard_hpid_to_ucde(). (4) Virtual printer creation, registration, and claiming via create_virtual_printer(), claim_virtual_printer(), create_and_claim_virtual_printer(), create_and_claim_virtual_printer_and_add_address(). (5) RTP device status simulation via send_rtp_devicestatus() which constructs complex binary telemetry payloads. (6) Offer code generation via offer_request(), get_offer(), get_offer_code(). (7) Payment method data management via get_payment_method_data() with dynamic expiration year calculation. (8) Subscription state validation via get_subscription_state(), validate_subscription_state(), validate_subscription_state_by_me(). (9) Email verification code retrieval via fetch_verification_code(), get_verification_code(), extract_verification_code(), new_extract_verification_code(). (10) Plan data retrieval via get_plans_data(), get_filtered_plan_data(), get_yearly_plans_data(), get_filtered_yearly_plan_data(). (11) UI element inspection and validation via get_font_details(), verify_font_details_property_value(), check_footer_fonts(), is_element_actionable(). (12) Date parsing utilities via parse_flexible_date(), extract_date_from_text(). (13) Payment engine and status code mapping via billing_status_code(), request_event_response_status(), refund_status(), payment_engine(). (14) Country-specific configuration via countries_without_states_mandate(), countries_with_direct_debit(). The file uses extensive global variables (prefixed with _) to maintain state across function calls. The STACK_CONFIG dictionary defines environment-specific endpoints for pie, stage, and production stacks including URLs for instantink, agena, cartridgepacker, fulfillmentservice, oss, hpid, stratus, ucde, and portalshell services. The module loads environment variables from .env file using dotenv. It configures a requests.Session with SSL verification disabled and proxy support for HP VPN. The PrinterData named tuple provides structured storage for printer information. The retry_operation() function provides generic retry logic used throughout the module. The send_rtp_devicestatus() function is particularly complex, constructing binary telemetry data using struct.pack() and base64 encoding, then loading JSON templates with placeholder replacement. The create_new_ii_v2_account() function is the most sophisticated UI automation function, handling multiple authentication flows including new account with password, new account with OTP, and existing account login with intelligent flow detection and branching. The module demonstrates advanced patterns including retry decorators (@retry), context managers (page.expect_response()), binary data manipulation (struct.pack/unpack), OAuth token exchange flows, and complex API orchestration. This file is absolutely critical to the test framework and would be heavily reused by any test that needs to create accounts, claim printers, generate offers, or interact with backend APIs.

key_reusable_methods: set_globals — Initialize all module-level global configuration variables from args dictionary and STACK_CONFIG
setup — Convenience setup function that initializes globals from GlobalState
generate_tenant_email — Generate unique test email using Gmail plus addressing
create_hpid — Create new HPID account through sign-up UI flow
create_ii_v2_account — Create new Instant Ink v2 account through complete sign-up flow
create_new_ii_v2_account — Enhanced account creation with support for password and OTP authentication flows
login_ii_v2_account — Log into existing Instant Ink v2 account with optional OOBE flow
logout_ii_v2_account — Log out of Instant Ink v2 account
fetch_verification_code — Fetch verification code from Gmail with automatic retry
get_verification_code — Retrieve verification code with 3 retry attempts and 15-second delays
onboard_hpid_to_ucde — Onboard HPID account to UCDE via OAuth flow and API calls
get_token_gemini_client — Obtain OAuth access token for Gemini client using client credentials
get_org_aware_token — Obtain organization-aware user access token through multi-step token exchange
create_virtual_printer — Create virtual printer through G2 simulator API with retry logic
claim_virtual_printer — Claim virtual printer to tenant account through UCDE API
create_and_claim_virtual_printer — Orchestrate complete printer creation and claiming flow
create_and_claim_virtual_printer_and_add_address — Create/claim printer and add shipping address
add_address_to_tenant — Add shipping address to tenant via Gemini comfe API
get_payment_method_data — Load payment method test data with dynamic expiration year calculation
send_rtp_devicestatus — Send RTP device status telemetry to G2 simulator
offer_request — Generate offer code from identifier via Agena API
get_offer — Retrieve offer from OFFERS_DATA matching specified criteria
get_offer_code — Generate offer code for current test country based on criteria
get_subscription_state — Extract subscription state from Gemini subscription data
validate_subscription_state — Validate subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds
get_plans_data — Retrieve all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API
get_filtered_plan_data — Retrieve plans filtered by attribute value
billing_status_code — Return expected billing status code based on payment method
payment_engine — Return payment engine name based on payment method and gateway
printer_colors — Return ink colors list based on supply variant (IPH or IIC)
parse_flexible_date — Parse date strings in multiple formats into datetime objects
extract_date_from_text — Extract and parse date from text containing mixed content
is_element_actionable — Check if element is actionable by verifying it's topmost at center point
get_font_details — Extract comprehensive font and text styling details from element
verify_font_details_property_value — Verify CSS property has expected value with assertion
retry_operation — Generic retry wrapper with configurable attempts, delay, and callback
test_data_files: locale_map.json — Country/locale mapping with ISO codes, languages, regions, payment gateways
offers.json — Offer configurations by country with GUIDs, identifiers, types, pricing
payment_method_data.json — Payment method test credentials for cards, direct debit, PayPal, Google Pay
printer_profiles.json — Printer SKU profiles by model and region with easy enrollment flags
key_configuration_constants: STACK_CONFIG — Environment-specific endpoint URLs for pie/stage/production stacks
DEFAULT_FIRSTNAME, DEFAULT_LASTNAME, DEFAULT_PASSWORD — Default test account credentials
GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET, GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN — Gmail API credentials
STRATUS_CLIENT_ID, UCDE_CLIENT_ID, GEMINI_CLIENT_ID — OAuth client IDs
MONGODB_URI, S3_ENDPOINT, S3_BUCKET — Database and storage configuration
RAILS_ADMIN_CONFIG — Rails admin endpoints and credentials
OFFERS_DATA — Loaded offers.json data for offer selection
api_endpoints_configured: instantink_url — Main Instant Ink application URL
agena_url — Agena service for plans, offers, OOBE registration
stratus_authz, stratus_tropos — Stratus authentication and API
ucde_authz, ucde_tropos, ucde_server — UCDE authentication and API
G2 simulator — https://g2sim.wpp.api.hp.com for virtual printer operations
